section.title: Research and writing
section.intro: Tools for literature search, synthesis, and drafting. The main risk is citation fabrication – dedicated tools search real databases, general-purpose models don't.

resources.title: Elicit
resources.author: elicit.com
resources.description: AI research assistant. 138 million indexed papers, systematic review mode. Searches real databases – does not generate citations.

resources.title: Semantic Scholar
resources.author: Allen Institute for AI
resources.description: Free academic search engine with AI-generated summaries, citation context, and research recommendations. Built by a nonprofit.

resources.title: Accuracy of AI-Generated References in Medical Literature
resources.author: JAMIA
resources.description: Found 26.5% of ChatGPT-generated references were fully correct. 64% of fabricated DOIs linked to real but unrelated papers.

resources.title: NotebookLM
resources.author: Google
resources.description: Google's research tool. 1 million token context, structured extraction, free. Best with curated source documents.

resources.title: Perplexity
resources.author: perplexity.ai
resources.description: AI search with academic filter and Deep Research mode. Produces cited responses. Verify citations against primary sources.

resources.title: Can Researchers Stop AI Making Up Citations?
resources.author: Nature
resources.description: Update on citation fabrication and the emerging "vibe citing" problem – researchers citing papers found via AI without reading them.

resources.title: Consensus
resources.author: consensus.app
resources.description: Answers plain-language research questions with synthesis from peer-reviewed papers, showing evidence behind each claim.

resources.title: Research Rabbit
resources.author: researchrabbit.ai
resources.description: Visual maps of related literature from seed papers. Finds connections that keyword search misses. Free.

resources.title: scite.ai – Smart Citations
resources.author: scite.ai
resources.description: Shows how a paper has been cited – whether subsequent papers supported, contradicted, or mentioned its findings.

resources.title: AMEE Guide No. 192: AI Tools in Scholarly Communication
resources.author: Medical Teacher
resources.description: AI disclosure obligations, appropriate uses, and ethical boundaries in research writing. Written for health professions.

resources.title: ATLAS.ti AI Coding
resources.author: ATLAS.ti
resources.description: ATLAS.ti, NVivo, and MAXQDA now embed generative AI for thematic analysis and coding. Participant data may be processed through AI models during analysis.
